---
title: Blue car observation
output: html_document
---

```{=html}
<script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.7.1.min.js" integrity="sha256-/JqT3SQfawRcv/BIHPThkBvs0OEvtFFmqPF/lYI/Cxo=" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)
```

```{js}
$(function() {
  $(".level2").css('visibility', 'hidden');
  $(".level2").first().css('visibility', 'visible');
  $(".container-fluid").height($(".container-fluid").height() + 300);
  $(window).on('scroll', function() {
    $('h2').each(function() {
      var h2Top = $(this).offset().top - $(window).scrollTop();
      var windowHeight = $(window).height();
      if (h2Top >= 0 && h2Top <= windowHeight / 2) {
        $(this).parent('div').css('visibility', 'visible');
      } else if (h2Top > windowHeight / 2) {
        $(this).parent('div').css('visibility', 'hidden');
      }
    });
  });
})
```

```{css}
.figcaption {display: none}
body{
  background-colour: #fde0dd;
    colour: #4a004a;
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
}
h1, h2 {
  colour: #c51b8a;
}
```

## visual data story

## Comparing light blue cars with total blue cars

![scatterplot relation ship between light blue cars and total blue cars]("plot2.png") plot2 shows that when the amount of cars seen increases so does the number of light blue cars seen. When total blue cars are between 5 and 15 the amount of light blue cars sits around 1-3.

## comparing time of day and total blue cars

![Bar chart of time of day and total blue cars seen comparing them]("plot1.png") the average of blue cars seen seems to be the most in the afternoon and evening, mornings have the least amount of blue car seen.

## patterns throughout the day

![line graph of average blue cars seen per day]("plot3.png") from this plot we can see my data has a huge gap which would have been when we wernt doing project 2 or 4, the most cars seen in total in one day was over 75.
